task_context.description: This is a comparative detector design and simulation task for high-energy physics instrumentation. The user wants to design and evaluate three different homogeneous electromagnetic calorimeter configurations using different crystal materials (PbWO4, BGO, and CsI) for a future electron-positron collider. The task involves running Geant4 Monte Carlo simulations to characterize detector performance metrics including energy resolution, shower containment properties, and spatial resolution. The analysis requires parameterizing energy resolution using the standard calorimeter formula, measuring shower development in both longitudinal and transverse dimensions, and determining optimal detector geometry requirements. This is specifically for precision electron and photon measurements at the proposed FCC-ee collider facility.
task_context.goal: Compare three crystal calorimeter materials and recommend the best choice for an FCC-ee electromagnetic calorimeter optimized for precision electron and photon measurements
constraints: Use homogeneous calorimeter design (no absorber material)
Limit to 3 configurations maximum (one per material)
Use tractable simulation parameters for practical computation
Test only electrons (e-) in particle gun
Require >99% energy containment for depth determination

data.output:
{'report': '# Comparative Study of Crystal Calorimeter Materials for FCC-ee Electromagnetic Calorimetry

## Abstract

This report presents a Monte Carlo simulation study comparing three homogeneous crystal calorimeter materials (PbWO₄, BGO, and CsI) for precision electromagnetic measurements at the proposed FCC-ee collider. Energy resolution, linearity, and containment properties were evaluated using Geant4 simulations with electron beams from 1-20 GeV.

## Methodology

Three homogeneous

observations.content: QUALITY GATE FAILED: deterministic physics violations (1). Extracted metrics: {'PbWO4_resolution_1GeV_percent': 1.56, 'PbWO4_resolution_20GeV_percent': 0.88, 'PbWO4_stochastic_term_percent': 0.83, 'PbWO4_constant_term_percent': 0.66, 'PbWO4_fit_R2': 0.85, 'PbWO4_containment_avg': 99.1, 'BGO_resolution_1GeV_percent': 1.59, 'BGO_resolution_20GeV_percent': 1.84, 'BGO_stochastic_term_percent': -0.2, 'BGO_constant_term_percent': 1.73, 'BGO_fit_R2': 0.32, 'BGO_containment_avg': 98.0, 'CsI_resolution_1GeV_percent': 5.72, 'CsI_resolution_20GeV_percent': 9.47, 'CsI_stochastic_term_percent': -4.87, 'CsI_constant_term_percent': 10.06, 'CsI_fit_R2': 0.89, 'CsI_containment_avg': 84.6, 'total_events_simulated': 60000, 'events_per_energy_point': 10000, 'hit_counts_total': '1.06B-1.07B per material'}. Verifier reasoning: All three crystal materials (PbWO4, BGO, CsI) were successfully simulated and analyzed. PbWO4 shows excellent performance with energy resolution 1.56% at 1 GeV improving to 0.88% at 20 GeV, good containment ~99%, and valid resolution parameterization (R²=0.85). BGO shows similar resolution but poor fit quality (R²=0.32) with negative stochastic term. CsI shows much worse resolution (5.7% at 1 GeV, 9.5% at 20 GeV) and poor containment (78-91%). All simulations used adequate statistics (10,000 events per energy point) and generated realistic hit counts (20M-400M hits). The comparative analysis correctly identifies PbWO4 as the best choice for FCC-ee based on quantitative scoring.
